name: Kawailoa
desc: Beachfront community between Haleiwa and Waimea — sprawling lots, surf access, rural North Shore living at its finest.
insider: The beachfront stretch has direct access to uncrowded surf breaks — locals paddle out from their yards
Lots are large and often agricultural-zoned — you'll see horses, chickens, and fruit trees everywhere
It's walking distance to Haleiwa town but feels completely rural and private once you're off the highway
The bike path along Kamehameha Highway is flat, scenic, and connects directly to Haleiwa shops and restaurants
Kawailoa Beach is known locally for consistent surf and far fewer people than Sunset or Pipeline
Many homes are old plantation-era houses on large ag lots — the renovated ones command serious premium prices
President Obama's vacation compound was in the Kawailoa Beach area — Secret Service presence was a regular sight for years
No streetlights on most roads — bring a flashlight at night and watch for pedestrians on Kam Highway
Fruit stands along the highway sell fresh papaya, banana, and coconut for almost nothing — always stop and buy local
The stretch between Haleiwa and Waimea Bay is one of the most scenic drives on the entire island
